settings.title: Settings
settings.daynightLinearChange: Linear transition between day and night
settings.minimalDehumidifierOffTimeHint: Minimum time in seconds the fridge/dehumidifier stays off before it can turn on again. Prevents short-cycling of the compressor.
settings.minimalDehumidifierOffTimeWarning: Warning: Values below 240s may damage the fridge compressor due to short-cycling!
settings.useLongHumidityAvg: Use long humidity average (240 instead of 100 values)

alarms.title: Alarms
alarms.upperThresholdHint: Alarm triggers when value goes above this
alarms.lowerThresholdHint: Alarm triggers when value goes below this
alarms.thresholdSecondsHint: Number of seconds the threshold must be exceeded before the alarm is triggered
alarms.actionTargetHint: Email address or webhook URL - if you want to use different targets for triggered and resolved alarms, separate them with a pipe '|' character. E.g. "http://example.com/turn-on|http://example.com/turn-off"
alarms.cooldownSecondsHint: How many seconds until the same alarm can be triggered again. For E-Mail actions, the minimum is 300.
alarms.retriggerSecondsHint: Seconds until the current alarm state is retriggered. In case an alarm state is not transmitted successfully, this ensures that the alarm state will be sent again after this time. (Minimum: 60)
alarms.name: Alarm Name
alarms.webhookMethodHint: Choose HTTP method for webhook requests.
alarms.webhookHeadersHint: Add custom HTTP headers to be sent with the webhook request.
alarms.webhookTriggeredPayloadHint: Custom payload sent when the alarm is triggered (for POST/PUT). Leave empty to send the default payload.
alarms.webhookResolvedPayloadHint: Custom payload sent when the alarm is resolved (for POST/PUT). Leave empty to send the default payload.
alarms.tunnelWebhookHint: If enabled, webhook requests will be tunneled through the module. This is helpful if your webhook URL is not directly reachable from the internet. WARNING: HTTPS may not work properly when tunneling is enabled.

settings.ppfdLuxFactorHint: PPFD is estimated as lux × this factor. It depends on the light spectrum (white LED ≈ 0.015, HPS ≈ 0.0122, sunlight ≈ 0.0185). Calibrate it to your grow light. Default 0.015.
settings.publicReadHint: If enabled, other people can view the charts page, diary page, and all webcam images when they have the link.
settings.rtspStreamHint: Enter the URL of your webcam here to enable webcam streaming in the portal, e.g. "rtsp://user:password@my-public-hostname-or-ip.com:554/". The input here must be processable by FFMPEG.
settings.maintenanceWebcamOffHint: If enabled, the webcam will automatically stop capturing images when the device is in maintenance mode.
settings.tunnelRtspStreamHint: If enabled, the webcam stream will be tunneled through the module. This is helpful if your webcam is not directly reachable from the internet.

charts.title: Charts
charts.logsNoEntries: No log messages for the selected timespan
charts.logsNoFilteredEntries: No log messages for the selected filters
charts.moreHiddenMessages: There are {{count}} more messages. Please reduce your selection.

fridge.title: Plantalytix FRIDGE GROW

settings.floating-day: Use custom duration for day/night cycle

fridge.manualModeOfflineWarning: If your device is offline, changes made in settings mode will not be applied until the device comes back online.

recipe.warning: Warning: Recipes rely on a steady internet connection. If the connection is lost, the recipe may not advance to the next step as expected.

co2inject.name: Automatic speed reduction
co2inject.description: This fan is paired to a Plantaltix smart socket in CO2 injection mode and its speed will automatically be reduced during CO2 injection

plug.title: Plantalytix SMART SOCKET
plug.co2sensor-text: This mode requires an optional CO2 sensor to work.

settings.heater-on-desc: Turns output ON when the measured temperature falls BELOW this value
settings.heater-off-desc: Turns output OFF when the measured temperature rises ABOVE this value
settings.cooler-on-desc: Turns output ON when the measured temperature rises ABOVE this value
settings.cooler-off-desc: Turns output OFF when the measured temperature falls BELOW this value
settings.humidify-on-desc: Turns output ON when the measured humidity falls BELOW this value
settings.humidify-off-desc: Turns output OFF when the measured humidity rises ABOVE this value
settings.dehumidify-on-desc: Turns output ON when the measured humidity rises ABOVE this value
settings.dehumidify-off-desc: Turns output OFF when the measured humidity falls BELOW this value

overtemperature.limit-desc: Socket will be disabled if measured temperature rises above this limit
overtemperature.hysteresis-desc: Temperature must fall this value below limit for socket to be reenabled

undertemperature.limit-desc: Socket will be disabled if measured temperature falls below this limit
undertemperature.hysteresis-desc: Temperature must rise this value above limit for socket to be reenabled

time.min_on-desc: Socket will not turn off for a minimum of this time after being turned on
time.min_off-desc: Socket will not turn on for a minimum of this time after being turned off

message-lost-connection-text: The connection between your device and our sever was lost and has been reestablished. If this messages appeares regulary please check your wifi and internet connection
message-device-booted-text: Your device has rebooted. If this messages appears regularly please check your power connection.
message-device-booted:POWERON-text: Your device has rebooted. The reason was a power cycle (POWERON) - this is normal after unplugging or a power outage. If this message appears unexpectedly please check your power connection.
message-device-booted:BROWNOUT-text: Your device has rebooted. The reason was a brownout: the supply voltage dropped below the safe threshold. This usually means the power adapter or USB cable cannot deliver enough current.
message-device-booted:TASK_WDT-text: Your device has rebooted. The reason was a task watchdog timeout: the main firmware loop did not respond in time and was force-restarted. This typically happens when network operations get stuck on a flaky internet uplink. If this message appears regularly please check your internet connection.
message-device-booted:INT_WDT-text: Your device has rebooted. The reason was an interrupt watchdog timeout: an interrupt routine in the firmware took too long. Please report this to support if it happens repeatedly.
message-device-booted:WDT-text: Your device has rebooted. The reason was an internal watchdog timeout. Please report this to support if it happens repeatedly.
message-device-booted:PANIC-text: Your device has rebooted. The reason was a firmware crash (panic) - the device restarted automatically. Please report this to support if it happens repeatedly.
message-device-booted:SW-text: Your device has rebooted. The reason was an intentional software-triggered restart (for example after a configuration change or firmware update).
message-device-booted:EXT-text: Your device has rebooted. The reason was a hardware reset via the reset pin.
message-device-booted:DEEPSLEEP-text: Your device has rebooted. The reason was a wake-up from deep sleep mode.
message-device-booted:SDIO-text: Your device has rebooted. The reason was a restart over the SDIO interface.
message-device-booted:UNKNOWN-text: Your device has rebooted. The reason was not reported by the device. If this message appears regularly please check your power connection and internet uplink.
message-device-booted:REMOTE-text: Your device has rebooted. The reason was an intentional restart triggered remotely via the portal.
message-buffer-overflow-text: Data could not be transfered to the server and was lost. Please check your internet connection or wifi.
message-device-firmware-update-text: The software of your device was updated
message-ext-sensor-deviate-text: External and internal sensor differ! Check sensor placement!
message-maintenance-mode-activated:0-tex: Your device's maintenance mode was manually disabled. Alarms will be re-enabled in 10 minutes.
message-maintenance-mode-activated-text: Your device was manually put into maintenance mode for {{value}} minutes. Alarms will be disabled during this time
message-maintenance-mode-activated-remot: Your device's maintenance mode was manually disabled via the portal. Alarms will be re-enabled in 10 minutes.
Your device was manually put into maintenance mode for {{value}} minutes via the portal. Alarms will be disabled during this time
message-firmware-update-complete-text: You device's firmware update was completed
message-firmware-update-complete-with-id: Your device's firmware update was completed: {{value}}
message-recipe-advanced-text: The recipe has advanced to step {{value}}.
message-recipe-looped-text: The recipe has looped back to step #1 ({{value}}).
message-recipe-completed-text: The recipe has completed all steps.
message-recipe-step-manually-activated-t: Recipe step {{value}} has been manually activated by the user.
message-rtsp-stream-error-text: Failed to fetch image from RTSP stream: {{value}}
message-smart-socket-cmd-failed-text: Failed to send a power command to the smart socket for role: {{value}}
message-smart-socket-connected-text: A smart socket was connected for role: {{value}}
message-smart-socket-disconnected-text: The smart socket assignment was removed for role: {{value}}
